que: What are Baseline Profiles used for?
options: Profile-guided optimization of important code paths

que: According to Android Developers, what is a common benefit of Baseline Profiles?
options: They can improve execution speed from first launch by about 30%
They reduce APK size to zero

que: Which runtime uses Baseline Profiles for optimization?
Which user journey is a strong candidate for a Baseline Profile?
options: A critical startup and home screen flow

que: What kind of performance issues can Baseline Profiles help reduce?
Why is it still important to benchmark after adding Baseline Profiles?
options: Because profiles do not replace measurement and validation
Because they only work in debug builds
Because Android requires benchmarks before every install

que: Which statement best describes how Baseline Profiles help first launch?
options: They avoid some interpretation and JIT work by giving ART useful profile data ahead of time
They eliminate the need for code execution

que: What is a common mistake with Baseline Profiles?
options: Keeping them aligned to critical user journeys
Validating performance changes after adding them
Profiling code users rarely execute and assuming the job is done

que: Can Baseline Profiles be shipped in apps or libraries?
Which official docs best support this topic?
options: Android Baseline Profiles overview and startup best practices
